items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Western Balkan countries

items.title: Municipalities4Democracy - Local youth policy and youth voice
items.summary: If you are a municipal youth policy officer and you want to improve your competences in engaging young people in democratic municipal decisions, then this training course is for you.

items.summary: Are you a youth worker supporting young women who face barriers to participation, information and access to opportunities?

items.title: Why municipal youth participation?
items.summary: The Council of Europe Youth Department, in the framework of the project Youth revitalising democracy, and the Democracy Reloading Partnership of Erasmus+ national agencies are organising a joint Seminar for municipal youth participation policy makers.
items.participants_countries: ANDORRA, MONACO, Morocco, SAN MARINO, Tunisia, United Kingdom, Eastern Partnership countries , Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Western Balkan countries

items.title: Mentoring under construction - Focus Mentoring for Organisations - training based on the Mentoring framework
items.summary: Are you eager to improve mentoring support within the European Solidarity Corps (ESC) programme? - Join us for the "Focus Mentoring", a residential training course that allows you to dive deep into the Mentoring Competence Framework.
items.participants_countries: Eastern Partnership countries , Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Southern Mediterranean countries , Western Balkan countries

items.title: Supporting Volunteer`s Mental Health Through Mentoring
items.summary: Mentoring is one of the key elements to ensure meaningful European Solidarity Corps volunteering projects. People who work directly with volunteers benefit from understanding better, how the volunteers’ mental health can be promoted and supported.
items.participants_countries: Eastern Partnership countries , Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Southern Mediterranean countries , Western Balkan countries

items.title: “All in” - Seminar for youth workers on strengthening democratic values and attitudes in local communities
items.summary: "All In" brings together youth work practitioners to explore the role of youth work in times of polarisation and growing challenges to democracy. Participants will exchange practices, strengthen democratic approaches, and develop concrete local actions
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

items.title: Volu.M.Entry - Basics and essentials for E.S.C projects management
items.summary: If you just landed on the European Solidarity Corps (E.S.C) planet, this training is the compass you need to start your journey!
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

items.title: Study Visit in the field of inclusion of migrant youth
items.summary: Observe frontline inclusion of migrant youth in Şanlıurfa. Visit local NGOs to analyze methodologies and build partnerships. With its massive displacement scale, the region offers a once-in-a-lifetime experience for youth practitioners.
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

items.title: How to talk about war
items.summary: The aim of the training is to develop the competences of youth workers to help them to engage young people in meaningful, informed, and sensitive conversations about wars and armed conflicts.
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

items.title: Mental Health in Youth Work - Training Course
items.summary: The training provides a foundation for understanding how youth workers can strengthen and support young people’s mental health, wellbeing, and resilience; aimed for youth workers who work directly with young people in youth work settings.
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

items.title: ID Talks: Social Sport
items.summary: The ID Talks are a series of 5 stand-alone online workshops on inclusion & diversity-related topics, with inspiring guest speakers, thought-provoking insights, small-group discussions and Q&A, not lasting more than 90 minutes each.
items.participants_countries: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries , Other countries in the world , Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

items.title: D.C.O.D.E. – Decoding Dopamine: A Strategic Map for Youth Workers
items.summary: D.C.O.D.E. is an Erasmus+ Training Course in Ighiu, Romania, for 25 youth workers from five countries. It explores dopamine, attention and digital habits, offering practical non-formal tools for healthier technology use.
items.participants_countries: Greece, Portugal, Romania, Spain, Türkiye

items.title: Harry Totter and the Facilitator's Stone
items.summary: Facilitation is more than leading activities. Explore how to create safe, meaningful and impactful learning experiences, develop your own facilitation style, and deepen your understanding of non-formal education.
items.participants_countries: Austria, Estonia, France, Germany, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Republic of North Macedonia, Serbia, Slovenia, Spain, Türkiye

items.title: WE NEET YOU: Seminar on Pathways for NEET Youth
items.summary: This seminar aims to strengthen the competences of youth workers by exploring the challenges faced by NEET youth, sharing good practices and discussing practical approaches to outreach, engagement and empowerment.

items.summary: We will focus on how different types of youth spaces can empower young people. Through study visits, workshops, participants will explore best practices.

items.title: "LIVING AND ORGANIZING IN OUR COMMUNITIES : Towards more justice, integrity, and tenderness"

items.title: Youth+: How to help youth with fewer/less opportunities grow in volunteering projects
items.summary: An online course for youth workers to learn: - how to approach a group of youth with fewer opportunities, - how to involve them, to create their own volunteering project to help a community in need, - how to train, coach, mentor and support them.

items.summary: How can international youth mobility become truly safe, inclusive, and empowering for Highly Vulnerable Young People? This self-paced course equips youth workers with practical tools, trauma-informed approaches, and safeguarding competencies for mobility.
